🌱 Bioregioning Earth
Organizations Sources Ontology How to Explore the Knowledge Commons About

Welcome to the Bioregional Knowledge Commons

This is a public knowledge base documenting organizations and practices at the frontier of bioregional work — exploring how communities around the world are learning to live and govern within the logic of place.

You can browse the map to explore where groups are practicing bioregional approaches, or click Table to see the database. For a detailed tour, visit How to Explore the Knowledge Commons.

110 Bioregional actors and initiatives currently mapped

View:
🗺️ Map 🗂️ Table

Bioregional Organizations

View all →

r3.0

r3.0 crowdsources open recommendations for necessary transformations across diverse fields and sectors, in response to the ecological and social collapses humanity is experiencing, in order to achieve

https://www.r3-0.org/

Yukon River Inter-Tribal Watershed Council

An Indigenous-led grassroots coalition founded in 1997, uniting 70+ First Nations and Alaska Native Tribes across the Yukon River watershed. Guided by a vision to one day safely drink water directly f

https://www.yritwc.org/

Yellowstone to Yukon Conservation Initiative (Y2Y)

The Yellowstone to Yukon Conservation Initiative (Y2Y) is a pioneering transboundary conservation network that unites governmental bodies, Indigenous groups, NGOs, scientists, landowners, and communit

https://y2y.net/

Wij land

In 2016, Wij.land started exploring how the 4 Returns can help restore ecological functions of the peat meadow landscape in the Vechtstreek. This has grown into a learning network of almost 200 cattl

https://wij.land/

Whanganui River

In New Zealand, the Whanganui River catchment forms the basis for both ecological governance and cultural identity for local Māori iwi (tribes). The recognition of the river as a legal person (Te Awa

Wellspring Commons

Wellspring Commons is a bioregionally grounded initiative dedicated to cultivating mutually thriving relationships between human communities and the larger web of life—from protecting ecological lifel

https://www.wellspringcommons.org/

Sources

View all →

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Vistula River Valley (Poland)

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Thermenlinie Wiener Becken (Austria)

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

Water Management Programme- Tarun Bharat Sangh

Case study detailing Tarun Bharat Sangh's johad-driven water revival and community governance model.

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Stroomgebied Zuid Veluwe (Netherlands)

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

Strengthening Southeast Alaska’s Businesses — Together

Feature on Spruce Root’s Native CDFI services delivering flexible capital, coaching, and seasonal-fit lending.

Resilience Circles helps Indigenous Entrepreneurs to Scale Their Business Up

Article on Alaska Native Resilience Circles blending business training, wellness, and cultural leadership.

Ontology

View all →

Universal_Basic_Income

Regular unconditional or broadly unconditional cash transfers that secure a baseline livelihood, widen economic agency in place, and complement local and regenerative economies—treating a floor of mea

Tag

Result_Based_Mechanisms

Financial mechanisms that compensate land stewards and projects based on measurable ecological outcomes—such as carbon sequestration, water purification, biodiversity conservation, or other ecosystem

Tag

Mutual_Credit_or_Exchange

Alternative systems where value is exchanged without conventional currency. Communities issue credit among members, fostering trust-based trade, local resilience, and circular economies.

Tag

Local_Currencies

Alternative currency systems that support local trade, retain value within the bioregion, and reflect place-based values.

Tag

Alternative Finance

Widening how value circulates in a bioregion—credit as trust, instruments that keep wealth and agency closer to home.

Theme

Investment_Vehicles

Funds, special purpose vehicles, and other instruments that pool capital, share risk, and channel investment into regenerative projects and enterprises.

Tag
Bioregioning Earth — Powered by Notion + Astro
